TextENGNew: ``...Heey, Minai.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2175. Have you finished the materials on that case yet?``
``Ah, yes.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2243. Here you are.``
Tomoe had just finished assembling some paperwork, and clipped it all together before presenting it to Maeno.
He flipped through it and perused the contents, then lightly nodded and stamped the cover.

``No problems here. Thank you for your work.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2177. ...But I must say, your handwriting is rather good.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2178. I'm used to documents written by women having thin lines... but your penmanship is clear and easy to read, like it's engraved on the paper.``
``...Is that a backhanded compliment?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2245. It somewhat feels like you're calling me manly.``

``Hmm? No, that isn't it at all.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2180. At the very least, it's far easier to read than my half-hieroglypic scribbles.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2181. That's why... I look forward to you handling copy writing in the future.``
``Much better... hehe.``
Tomoe responded with a smile and a laugh, then immediately spread open another file and began working on her next task.
She had a fast pace while doing clerical work, and on top of that she was able to grasp written material very quickly.
Thanks to that, the time spent preparing documents for submission was significantly reduced, and the stagnant office work was progressing far faster than before.
(But... there is no way I could've expected her to finish the work I gave her back then.)
Maeno recalled a week ago, when he left Tomoe some work to do and headed out.

Even though she was halfway to exhaustion and had dark circles under her eyes, her pupils were full of a fierce light that left quite the impression on him when she presented him with the stack of material...
After giving that order, he headed to an off-track horse racing facility in Shinjuku, then went home, changed his clothes, attended some meetings, and finally returned to the office around 3 AM.
``...Thanks for all the hard work.``
Alone in that large room... Tomoe raised her head and turned toward Maeno, with a pile of paper cups on her desk giving off the smell of coffee.
``Oh, thank you too.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2184. ...It's your first day on the job, so why not start things out by leaving on time?``
``I can't leave until the work is done.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2249. And I can't take the case files home with me.``
``...How diligent.``
Despite Maeno's teasing, he walked into the room expecting she would still be here.
At the very least, he got the impression from their previous interview that she was not one to set work aside half-complete, or to head home without first reporting to her boss.
(Well, she is passed the first test...@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2187. And...)
``The documents... are all done.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2251. Here, please give them a look.``
He accepted the two large volumes of documents and quickly scanned through the contents.
...And then, Maeno uttered a groan of astonishment.
(...She really did it.)
...If she had finished by first thing in the morning, it would've been a great accomplishment.
And even if she hadn't finished it all by then, Maeno would've given her a passing score.
But Tomoe had finished the work this early, without even knowing if he would be coming back or not.
She was probably factoring in time to incorporate feedback and corrections from her boss.
And what's more, the content itself was nearly perfect...
(Even though I intentionally mixed the pages up... she read it well and was able to do it.)
Maeno was certain... she would be valuable here.
Someone with extreme pride in their work as a detective would have seen writing copy as an insult or a punishment, and would not have had any motivation to carry it out.
They'd just copy the words as written without even reading the contents. It is a test of patience more than anything.
But Tomoe carefully scrutinized it by reading the surrounding context... so even the poorly scribbled notes were transcribed accurately.
It is not just about having an earnest or hardworking personality.
It is something impossible to do without strong pride and belief in your work.
``So... how is it?``
``Right... this is good.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2193. I appreciate all the effort you put into this.``
``Thank you.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2254. Then, with this, I...``
``...Chief Inspector Minai.``
As Tomoe rose to her feet, Maeno smiled and pointed at the back door to the left.
It was a ``reward`` he had personally prepared in case she passed the test.
``The room back there's a nap room.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2196. ...Try to get a little sleep.``
``Don't worry, I cleaned it out for you.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2198. You can lock it from the inside, and it shouldn't have that weird stench in there.``
``...Maeno-san.``
``We're starting bright and early tomorrow, too... so be ready moving forward.``
After saying that, Maeno began to leave the room with the files in hand.
...As he turned away, he could see Tomoe's reflection in the glass of the door, deeply bowing down.
(I knew it... they are exactly the same.)
Maeno muttered that to himself and raised his hand as he left the area.
It seemed like securing the nap room was going to be a difficult task in the future.
...Even so, that thought made him elated instead.
changeReason: New translation 'elated' vs 'happy' — 嬉しい means happy/glad; 'elated' is a stronger emotion, shifting the reader's understanding of intensity.

TextENGNew: ...And today marked one week since that day.

It seemed like Tomoe had already become completely familiar with the Public Relations Office. She brought in her own coffee maker (for odor mitigation), and worked on tasks for Maeno one after another, clearing the place up without any hesitation.
It is true that she was not intimately familiar with Public Relations, so she sometimes made mistakes and had to be careful about certain things.
...But she listened obediently and always paid attention in order to avoid making the same mistake twice.
And since she never missed her interim reports, as a boss he was left with peace of mind.
(...She is something else.)
Of course, as someone who had been working as a detective up until now, she still had her regrets and lingering attachments.
And even if she never said it, it was easy to imagine she was baffled by the transfer.
But even so, Tomoe never overlooked anything or showed any signs of laziness.
In fact, she was enthusiastically working in her current role, treating it as something new to try out.
With all that dedication, he was frankly impressed by her ambitious personality.
``...Oh by the way, Minai.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2203. A letter came for you.``
``A letter...?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2258. Oh, it must be from my grandmother.``
After saying that, Tomoe took the letter from Maeno, checked the sender on the back, and nodded as if to say ``I knew it.``
And when she opened it, one slip of paper and several sheets of stationery came from inside.
``What is that?``

``It's an insurance collection stub.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2260. I had it sent to me because year-end adjustments are coming up soon, but it was hard to guess when I'd actually be home to receive it.@k@|@y@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2261. ...Please forgive me for using the workplace mail system for personal business.``

``No, it's fine.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2206. It's certainly true that you've been staying here overnight these days... and I'm the one responsible for that.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2207. ...But more importantly,``
Maeno suddenly asked a quick question when he noticed the destination line on the envelope.

``You shouldn't have to write your name in hiragana like that. I don't think anyone would mistake it.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2209. I can't imagine there are many people who don't know ``Tomoe`` is the reading for that kanji.``
``...Oh, that isn't it.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2263. My real name is actually written in hiragana.``
``Back when I was in school, I always wrote ``Tomoe`` in hiragana.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2265. It was only after I entered the police academy that I switched to writing it in kanji.``
``I see.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2212. ...However, why would you switch from the name your parents gave you?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2213. Oh, sorry... that was rude of me to ask.``
Thinking he was prying too deep into personal territory, Maeno raised his hands and said ``sorry,`` intending to cut off this line of conversation.
However, Tomoe thought for a moment, then gradually shook her head and interrupted him.

``...@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2267.Back when I was taking the police academy entrance exam, my grandmother told me.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2268. My father really wanted my name to be written with the kanji for ``Tomoe.``@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2269. I was apparently named after the female hero from the Genpei War, Tomoe Gozen.``
One of the generals serving under Genji was Minamoto no Yoshinaka... and his wife was Tomoe Gozen.
They say she even dressed in armor and wielded a longsword to fight her foes.
So, he wanted his first-born daughter to be a woman with a strong will and a courageous heart.
And that was why her father had decided to name his daughter ``Tomoe.``
``But... it seems Mom didn't like that idea.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2271. She wanted me to grow up as a cute girl, rather than a heroic one.``

``Plus, they felt it was better to have a name that's easy for school friends to read, one that wouldn't invite any weird nicknames.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2273. So they settled on writing it in hiragana.``
It was the same with her sister Madoka. In fact, he wished to use the kanji for yen when writing her name, since it came from her mother's name.
...Even if those were trivial considerations, Tomoe could tell how much her parents loved her, and felt deeply grateful.
(Still... what I need right now is neither kindness nor femininity.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2275. It is a strong will, courage... and fulfilling my father's dying wish...!)
That is why... Tomoe changed her name to the one her father gave her.
It was proof of her determination.
And in a sense, it was like a ritual for hammering an indomitable will into herself.
``......@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2215.That's just like Nobumi-san.``

``...Come to think of it, Minai,@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2217. the Secretariat of the Commissioner is interested in speaking with you.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2218. He wanted to offer you his encouragement as a fellow alum from Kashima University.``
``The... Secretariat?``
``Yes.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2220. ...So there's something I'd like to ask you.``
